Bash(gh pr create --title "Refactor: split optimization.py into focused modules" --body "$\(cat <<''EOF''
## Summary
- **Split 1,612-line `optimization.py`** into 4 focused modules \(`objectives.py`, `results.py`, `threshold.py`, `dashboard.py`\) while keeping `optimization.py` as the core Optuna orchestration with backward-compatible re-exports
- **Fixed bug** in `validation.py` line 817: undefined `conditions_list` → `condition_grid`
- **Added deprecation warning** to legacy `extract_calibration_metrics\(\)` pointing to `run_validation_pipeline\(\)`
- **Cleaned up `diagnostics.py`**: removed dead duplicate `plot_coverage_diff`, extracted `_create_condition_grid\(\)` and `_hide_empty_subplots\(\)` helpers to DRY up 5 grid-plot functions

## Module breakdown

| Module | LOC | Responsibility |
|--------|-----|----------------|
| `objectives.py` | ~240 | Parameter counting, normalization, objective computation |
| `results.py` | ~330 | Optuna trial analysis, Pareto front, visualization |
| `threshold.py` | ~280 | Threshold-based training loop \(Optuna-independent\) |
| `dashboard.py` | ~120 | Optuna Dashboard CLI launcher |
| `optimization.py` | ~690 | Core Optuna study creation, hyperparameter sampling, objective factory |

## Backward compatibility
All existing imports continue to work:
```python
# This still works — optimization.py re-exports everything
from rctbp_bf_training.core.optimization import get_pareto_trials
```

## Test plan
- [x] `pytest tests/` passes \(1 test\)
- [x] `ruff check` — no new issues \(only pre-existing UP035/UP006 patterns\)
- [x] Package import test validates module loading

Bash(.venv/Scripts/python.exe -u -c " # -*- coding: utf-8 -*- import os; os.environ[''KERAS_BACKEND''] = ''torch'' import sys; sys.stdout.reconfigure\(encoding=''utf-8''\) import torch torch.set_grad_enabled\(True\) import numpy as np import keras from bayesflow_irt import \( EquivariantIRTSummary, EquivariantIRTApproximator, Prior, make_1pl_simulator, make_1pl_adapter, probe_memory_config, \) import bayesflow as bf PRIOR_B = Prior\(''normal'', loc=0, scale=2.0\) N_ITEMS = \(10, 30\) N_PERSONS = \(50, 100\) BATCH_SIZE = 256 sim = make_1pl_simulator\(n_items=N_ITEMS, n_persons=N_PERSONS, prior_b=PRIOR_B\) adapter = make_1pl_adapter\(prior_b=PRIOR_B, pad_to_2d=False\) summary_net = EquivariantIRTSummary\( embed_dim=16, num_inner_sabs=1, num_outer_sabs=1, inner_embed_dim=16, outer_embed_dim=16, inner_mlp_width=32, outer_mlp_width=32, \) flow = bf.networks.FlowMatching\(\) approx = EquivariantIRTApproximator\( adapter=adapter, inference_network=flow, summary_network=summary_net, \) approx.compile\(optimizer=''adam''\) # Count weights before probe n_weights_before = len\(approx.get_weights\(\)\) print\(f''Model weights before probe: {n_weights_before}''\) # Run probe \(same as notebook\) print\(''Running probe...''\) probe_report = probe_memory_config\( approx, make_1pl_simulator, batch_size=BATCH_SIZE, n_items=N_ITEMS, n_persons=N_PERSONS, verbose=True, \) n_weights_after = len\(approx.get_weights\(\)\) print\(f''Model weights after probe: {n_weights_after}''\) # Train steps after probe print\(f''\\nTraining steps AFTER probe \(batch_size={BATCH_SIZE}\)''\) losses = [] for i in range\(20\): sample = sim.sample\(\(BATCH_SIZE,\)\) adapted = approx.adapter\(sample\) metrics = approx.train_step\(adapted\) loss_val = float\(metrics[''loss'']\) losses.append\(loss_val\) print\(f''Step {i+1:2d} loss: {loss_val:.4f}''\) print\(f''\\nMean loss \(last 10\): {np.mean\(losses[-10:]\):.4f}''\) ")
